findings.finding: The 500-character cap is measured differently by Task 2 and Task 3 — Task 3's ~80-char truncation suffix is appended after the cap, so head -c 500 + suffix produces a ~580-char field that fails Task 2's wc -c ≤ 500 assertion.
findings.evidence_quote: Replace the head -20 truncation at L45 with a byte-cap that limits the additionalContext payload to ≤ 500 characters on both success and failure paths, appending an explicit ... [truncated; run `just test-skill-design test-skill-contracts` for full output] suffix when truncation occurs.

findings.finding: Depends on: [1, 2] on Task 3 models the relationship as a sequencing constraint, but the real coupling is a shared-contract dependency — no fixture, example argv, or schema both tasks can target. The only forcing function exposing drift is Task 3's final AC; no decision rule for whether to bend the test or the hook on disagreement.

Task 4's grep -c 'exit.*[0237]' ≥ 4 AC does not enforce its stated intent of four distinct exit-code branches. The character class [0237] matches any single digit in that set; grep -c counts lines, so the AC passes on four prose lines containing 'exit' plus any of 0/2/3/7, without enumerating the exit-0/2/3/64/70 branches.
findings.evidence_quote: `grep -c 'exit.*[0237]' skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md` ≥ 4 — pass if count ≥ 4 (one match per resolver exit-code branch).

findings.finding: Task 6's claim that test_skill_descriptions.py enforces atomic fixture+description removal is asymmetric. The test fails when a fixture-pinned phrase is missing from the SKILL.md description but does NOT fail when a phrase remains in the description after being removed from the fixture. Description-only leftover goes silent.
findings.evidence_quote: removing 'find gaps in requirements' from the fixture AND from the description must happen atomically in the same commit or one of the two tests will fail.

findings.finding: Spec's Rollback Playbook advertises four-file recovery (auto-scan.md, discovery/SKILL.md, skill_trigger_phrases.yaml, docs/interactive-phases.md) but Task 5's verification only checks one file at the tag. An operator who reorders or mis-pins would still pass the AC while shipping a broken recovery affordance.
findings.evidence_quote: `git cat-file -e deprecated-auto-scan-2026-05-11:skills/discovery/references/auto-scan.md` — pass if exit 0 (tagged commit still contains the file).

findings.finding: If the operator rebases the branch (e.g., to resolve main conflicts during review), the Sub-item 1 commit's SHA changes and the tag becomes pinned to a now-orphan pre-rebase commit. No AC distinguishes a healthy tag on a live commit from an orphan tag on a dangling commit. Synthesizer downgraded from A: plan's stated outcome (tag exists pointing to commit containing auto-scan.md) still holds against the orphan; recovery still works if tag is fetched. Adjacent verification gap.
findings.evidence_quote: Create an annotated git tag deprecated-auto-scan-2026-05-11 pointing to HEAD (which at this point in the commit sequence is the Sub-item 1 commit and still contains skills/discovery/references/auto-scan.md).
